ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Mar-03-2021 Continued To Jul-21-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
Graham Douds for Trustee.
Jim Chambers for Respondent.

Mr. Chambers stated a comprehensive settlement offer was sent to the other side, that there are remaining issues still pending, and that it's going to take time as there is a long list. The court asked if parties are interested in assistance from the court's mediation program. Mr. Chambers stated parties conducted private mediation with Judge Whitley, that Judge Whitley remains available if needed, that he is cautiously optimistic having framed the issues and everyone agrees they should settle. Mr. Douds stated that parties are at an impasse, that he would like to get a date on calendar for parties to press the matter forward and asked the court to grant an order for those issues. Mr. Douds further stated that they intend to move forward with the sale, that there will be a notice of proposed action to list the property and believe the property will be sold. The court stated it does not agree this matter is at the stage to be set for a trial date, that the probate examiner's notes need to be addressed, that there is also the issue of whether the 3rd amendment to the trust is valid raised in previous examiner's notes, and that the trustee will need to submit timesheets if she want court approval for her fees. Mr. Douds stated that he believed timesheets were only needed if it was for hourly rates and not needed for percentage basis. The court stated that the 1% fee is one method and hourly another method, but a timesheet will give them an idea of how much time was put into the case to determine if the 1% is reasonable, and in addition, for Mr. Douds to provide a verified declaration explaining titling and balance discrepancy for the account ending in 5583. Both counsels stated they anticipate a few months for settlement discussions and discovery. The court informed counsels of options to drop off ex parte request or jointly contact Elizabeth Kelber for referral to court's judicial mediation program.

The amended first account and report of trustee and petition for settlement; etc. is continued to July 21, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for case management.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Feb-03-2021 Continued To Mar-03-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Jan-11-2021 Continued To Feb-03-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Nov-02-2020 Continued To Jan-11-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Graham Douds, Esq., counsel for Ellen Gee, Trustee (appearing by telephone); Timothy Chambers, Esq. for Terry Gee, Respondent (appearing by video conference).

At the Court's query, Mr. Douds reports on the need for an Order to distribute certain property of the trust since the trust terms already provide for the same. Mr. Chambers responds. Court and counsel discuss the want of settlement and the remaining impediments to settlement, including the distribution of the 42nd Avenue property. Counsel offer that evidentiary hearing set in the Spring or Summer of 2021 may be in order. The Court defers consideration of the same at this time and continues this proceeding to January 11, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for further status and review.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Sep-21-2020 Continued To Nov-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Graham Douds appeared by telephone for Ellen Gee.
David Thompson appeared by zoom for Terry Gee.

Mr. Douds requested a 60-day continuance stating that the parties have agreed to have mediation on October 6, 2020. Mr. Thompson agrees. The First Account and Report of Trustee and Petition for Its settlement, etc. is continued to November 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Court Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Not reported.
